name: Apple & Rye Bread Pudding
recipeIngredient: 5 1/2 cups Cubed fresh or stale sourdough, wheat, or rye bread
recipeInstructions:
1. Cube the bread and toss in a large bowl with raisins and caraway seeds. Stale bread may be used instead of fresh.
2. Toss apples in a bowl with bread cubes.
3. Combine apple, sunflower butter (or tahini), 1 cup water, miso paste, amazake or soy milk in a separate bowl until well combined.
4. Pour the liquid mixture over the apples and bread. Stir to coat evenly.
5. Spoon the mixture into a lightly oiled 2-quart casserole dish or two 9-inch pie plates or cake pans. Cover with an oven-safe lid.
6. Allow the mixture to sit for 2 to 4 hours so that the bread can soak up flavors.
7. Bake covered at 350°F for 1 hour, or until slightly firm and the mixture pulls away from the sides slightly.
8. Remove from oven and spoon into individual dessert cups using an ice cream scoop.
9. Allow to cool slightly before serving.
